mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth provides the broadest measure of economic expansion; sustained positive GDP growth raises corporate earnings expectations and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 4-quarter GDP growth below 0% coincides with positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals improving labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns after UNRATE falls below 6.0% are not statistically significantly positive (one-sided t-test p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the inflation rate, real interest rates are negative, providing accommodative monetary conditions that compress discount rates and encourage risk-taking, boosting equity prices.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below the 12-month CPI inflation rate (CPIAUCSL year-over-year change)
failure_condition: Negative real rate regimes (FEDFUNDS < CPI inflation) produce 20-day SPX returns with a median below zero or rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment (above 6%), strong monthly payroll gains signal that the labor market slack is being absorbed, raising expectations of a durable recovery and boosting equity prices in the near term.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200,000 when UNRATE > 6%
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns following strong payroll prints (PAYEMS > 200k) in high-unemployment regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and business investment, which tends to precede broader economic growth and corporate profit recovery, supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO monthly change and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is negative or not statistically significant at the 5% level over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly growth in retail and food services sales reflects robust consumer demand, which directly influences corporate revenues and can lead to positive short-term equity market reactions.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns following RSAFS growth > 1% are not significantly above the unconditional mean return (permutation test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and retail sales signals broad-based demand momentum that reinforces each other, creating a virtuous cycle of construction, spending, and employment that boosts equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST (annualized) > 1200k and RSAFS monthly level > 420,000 million
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative in top-quintile joint HOUST/RSAFS regimes, or the signal's rank IC is below 0.01 over any 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI year-over-year below 2%), a rise in inflation toward 2% signals improving demand without an immediate tightening response, which reduces deflation fears and supports equity prices.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in a majority of cases where CPI crosses from below 2% to above 2%, or the average return is not statistically different from zero.
